meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop drowning in tutorials. Learn how to implement analytics and validate your SaaS MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code to drive real revenue today.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start measuring. By implementing event-based analytics early, you transform your MVP from a guessing game into a data-driven engine. Use Antigravity to structure your data schema and Claude Code to automate the integration, ensuring you track actual user behavior and conversion metrics immediately.
content.intro:
The screen glows with the fifth 'Build a To-Do App' tutorial of the week, but your bank account remains empty. You are trapped in the endless loop of passive consumption, trading your most valuable asset—time—for the illusion of progress. This isn't learning; it is a sophisticated form of procrastination that keeps you from building a product people will actually pay for.

To break free, you must switch from student to architect. The antidote to tutorial hell is shipping, and the catalyst for shipping is data. By integrating professional-grade analytics into your MVP today, you force yourself to confront the only metric that matters: user retention. We will use Antigravity and Claude Code to move from theory to a deployed, trackable SaaS component in minutes.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the single user action that defines value. For a SaaS, this is usually 'Account Created' or 'First Project Deployed'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a SaaS analytics tracking plan. Identify the top 3 user events that indicate high-intent conversion and define their required properties.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create an analytics_schema.json file based on the defined conversion events'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Initialize your analytics SDK to prepare your environment for event ingestion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable analytics wrapper for a Next.js application using PostHog or Segment, including error handling and event buffering.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the analytics wrapper in lib/analytics.ts and integrate into the main layout'

steps.title: Instrument High-Value Events
steps.description: Map your North Star metrics to specific UI components to capture user intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom React hook 'useTrackEvent' that wraps our analytics provider and makes it easy to fire events from any button click or form submission.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply useTrackEvent to the primary signup button and the project creation modal'

steps.title: Automate User Context Enrichment
steps.description: Capture user metadata like plan type or sign-up date to segment your data effectively.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a function to identify users upon login, passing attributes like email, plan_tier, and current_org_id to the analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Update the auth flow to trigger identify() on successful login'

steps.title: Validate the Data Pipeline
steps.description: Ensure events fire correctly in a staging environment before deploying to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a test script that simulates user navigation and clicks to verify that events are hitting the analytics dashboard with the correct payload.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run the analytics verification test suite and display the output'

pro_tips: Never track everything. If everything is important, nothing is. Focus exclusively on events that correlate with revenue or churn.
Use 'Anonymous ID' persistence to track users before they sign up; this reveals the friction points in your landing page funnel.
Implement a 'Debug Mode' flag in your config that logs events to the browser console during development to save time.
Avoid vendor lock-in by using a thin abstraction layer (the wrapper we built) so you can switch analytics providers as you scale.
Automate your reporting by setting up a weekly summary email of your North Star metric to keep your focus on growth, not just vanity traffic.
content.next_steps: Now that your tracking is live, push your MVP to production and share it with 10 potential users. Don't look at the code—look at the analytics dashboard to see where they get stuck.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start building for growth. Leverage the tools that elite founders use to iterate faster and validate their ideas with cold, hard data. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
